name: Bob Moses
abstract: Robert Laurence Moses (born January 28, 1948) is an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Gary Burton Quartet

works.t: Let's Face the Music and Dance
Sophisticated Ladies: It Don't Mean a Thing
I Get Along Without You Very Well

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Friends of ours: Jack Gregg, Bob Moses... Bob Moses and I were good friends... So we though the idea of having a cooperative band... would be a great idea.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: And Bob and I had worked in L.A. together backing up a folk singer named Chad Mitchell. And so we had become really good friends... Bob was like an older brother to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: That was the one with Bob Moses, Larry Coryell, Steve Swallow and Gary. And we practiced it, we rehearsed it out in California I remember, and recorded it in New York.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: So it was, uh, Kuhn, Steve Kuhn, Bob Moses, who I love… talk about a genius, man. ... So it was Kuhn, me, Moses, uh, and Harvey... So there were five of us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: we ended up with Bob Moses, who was a friend of Larry’s—so he ended up in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Bob Moses, who became my first real playing friend, and who was way ahead of me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
